turnaround_reason: Insperity was crushed by SMB employment softness, healthcare cost inflation pressuring PEO margins, and a competitive Workday partnership investment dragging earnings. The Q1 beat and price recovery to the 200-day MA suggest the operating reset is taking hold.
bullish_points: Just reclaimed 200-day MA (+0.6%) with strong 16% 30-day and 5-day momentum — recovering setup
Q1 EPS beat +6.2% after two prior misses — inflection point
$555M cash vs $430M debt — net cash position
Forward P/E 13.1x reasonable for a profitable services franchise
4 analysts with mean target $39.50 (+8% upside) — modest but supportive
bearish_points: Zero insider open-market buying despite -43% drawdown — director grants only
Two consecutive earnings missed badly before Q1 (Q4 -28.9%, Q3 -190%)
Revenue growth only 1.7% YoY — SMB market remains soft
EV/EBITDA 85x reflects compressed earnings (margins not yet recovered)
Operating margin only 3.3%, profit margin -0.4% (TTM loss)
Held by institutions 110% (>100% means heavily owned including short borrows) — crowded
Workday partnership investment may continue to drag near-term results
catalyst: Q2 earnings demonstrating continued margin recovery and SMB hiring stabilization
suggested_trade: Spot only — options have low OI (185 calls, 655 puts) and thin pricing. Wait for pullback to $32-33 area for better entry
key_risk: SMB employment weakens further; healthcare cost trend reaccelerates compressing PEO margins; Q2 reverts to a miss
would_skip_if: Q2 misses or guides down; SMB employment data weakens; rally continues without insider buys to confirm
